description: After seven people died in the Chicago area due to cyanide-laced Tylenol, Johnson & Johnson faced a critical choice: defend the product or recall all inventory. They chose a historic full-market withdrawal that became the gold standard for corporate crisis management.

key_events.description: News breaks about poisoned Tylenol; public panic ensues.
J&J announces a total national recall of 31 million bottles.
J&J introduces industry-first triple-sealed tamper-evident packaging.
Market share recovers nearly to pre-crisis levels.

calibration_notes: Useful for assessing the 'recovery phase' of public opinion after decisive corporate action.
